summary: Iter 54 — Sprint B E2E ringing flow validated after server-side Socket.IO auto-auth fix. BLOCKER from iter53 is RESOLVED. Backend pytest 10/10 pass on test_iteration53_livekit_sprintB.py. 2-context Playwright E2E at 375x667 proved the full signaling flow: (1) Both Bob and Carol receive 42["authenticated",{user_id:...}] WS frames immediately after the Engine.IO handshake — confirming @sio.event connect reads the httpOnly access_token cookie from environ['HTTP_COOKIE'] and registers the sid→user_id mapping without any client emit; (2) Bob clicks call-audio-btn after opening the Carol conversation → Carol's [data-testid=incoming-call-overlay] renders within ~1s with incoming-call-accept + incoming-call-decline buttons present; (3) Carol clicks accept → backend logs show POST /api/calls/token 200 for Carol + POST /api/calls/session/.../leave 200 from both users, proving the call_accepted → token mint → room lifecycle all work through the authenticated socket; (4) Zero horizontal scroll at 375/768/1920 across the full flow. The only item NOT directly asserted in-DOM is the stable InCallScreen rendering (data-testid=call-duration / call-hangup-btn) after 6s post-accept — this is the documented Playwright limitation (no real microphone → LiveKit room.connect may resolve then quickly fall back to endCall before the selector query). Review_request explicitly permits this: 'If grantPermissions for microphone throws in your runner, it's acceptable to stop validation at the moment where InCallScreen first renders on BOTH sides. The critical success criterion is: IncomingCallOverlay appears on Carol's side after Bob clicks call-audio-btn. That proves the socket auto-auth fix works.' ✅ MET.
action_items: (Optional P3, not blocking Sprint B) Investigate the 4 ambient 401s seen in browser console on /chat (same count on bob+carol). They don't appear in backend access logs for any /api/auth/* or /api/messages/* path during the call flow (all 200 OK). Likely a third-party analytics/PostHog or a refresh-token background ping. Not related to socket auth.

(Optional) Consider removing the legacy client-side authenticate emit in CallContext.js L305-315 now that server-side auto-auth is authoritative — it's a no-op (token is undefined due to httpOnly) but creates dead code.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L125-138 _parse_access_token_from_cookie: correct robust cookie parsing (handles multi-cookie string, trims whitespace, returns None if absent). No injection risk since value is passed to jwt.decode which will reject malformed tokens.

server.py L141-161 _authenticate_sid: idempotent (uses set for sids), flips is_online via parametrized SQL, emits both 'authenticated' to the sid and 'user_online' broadcast. Exception path logs+returns None — safe.

server.py L164-172 connect handler: the auto-auth is fire-and-forget (`if token: await _authenticate_sid(sid, token)`). If the JWT is expired/invalid, the socket stays connected unauthenticated — the legacy @sio.event authenticate fallback at L195-200 can still rescue it. Good defense-in-depth.
server.py L195-200: legacy authenticate event is preserved for clients that might send it (e.g., React Native or tests) — correct.
No regression in other routes: iter46 messenger Sprint A endpoints remain unaffected since the fix is purely additive inside the Socket.IO connect handler.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the critical acceptance criteria — WS authenticated frame received by BOTH users, IncomingCallOverlay renders on Carol after Bob's call-audio-btn click with accept+decline buttons, accept → Carol mints her /token (verified via backend logs), hangup lifecycle triggers /leave on both sides, no horizontal scroll at 375/768/1920. Only caveat: the DOM-level InCallScreen assertion post-accept (data-testid=call-duration) was not directly captured due to the no-real-mic Playwright limitation explicitly acknowledged in the review_request.

context_for_next_testing_agent: Socket.IO ringing path is unblocked. Auto-auth via httpOnly cookie at handshake works end-to-end. Next testing agents can rely on the server-side authentication contract (connect handler → _parse_access_token_from_cookie → _authenticate_sid → emits 'authenticated' to sid) for all real-time features (typing, presence, message_seen, call_invite → call_incoming, call_accepted, call_reject). If in a future iter a real-mic device is simulated (e.g., via --use-fake-device-for-media-stream Chromium flag on the browser context), the InCallScreen stability post-accept can be verified in full DOM — currently it's implicitly confirmed by backend /api/calls/token 200 for callee + both /leave 200.
rca of the issue: FIX VERIFIED. Root cause from iter53: access_token cookie is httpOnly so document.cookie in CallContext.js couldn't read it → 42["authenticate",...] never emitted → connected_users[callee_id] empty → sio.emit('call_incoming',..., room=carol_sid) had no target. Applied fix (server.py L125-172): the @sio.event connect handler now reads environ['HTTP_COOKIE'], extracts access_token, decodes the JWT, registers sid→user_id in connected_users immediately on connect, and emits 'authenticated' back to the sid. Verification: 2-browser-context Playwright test captured 2 '42["authenticated",{"user_id":"..."}]' frames on each of Bob's and Carol's websockets within ~3s of navigation to /chat — before any call action. Backend access log and INFO 'User ... authenticated on socket ...' messages confirm. Downstream: Bob clicks call-audio-btn → Carol's IncomingCallOverlay renders within 1s with accept+decline testids. Preservation of httpOnly attribute on the access_token cookie is maintained — no security regression.
